all_reviews: [4.0] [8 years ago on Zomato]: Absolutely love this place. I stumbled upon this place on a hungry morning and decided to try their Egg wrap which was really delicious. Recommended || [5.0] [8 years ago on Zomato]: The food is amazing here, we tried the kaake chicken burger and we loved it and we loved the potato with the dipping sauces also ! Great decoration and friendly staff. A must try restaurant! || [5.0] [8 years ago on Zomato]: We have personally known Abdo since forever and we were delighted to learn that his long studied dough has finally a place to shine! The passion behind the place is clear, Abdo restaurant is composed of a team of young men including M3alim Abdo, passionate about their food and who have developed their special dough into a unique piece of art that cannot be found anywhere else in Lebanon. It may seem as your local bakery shop, but its not, the taste of Saj, Mne2ich and Kaak you get at this place beats any you ever had before. The dough is prepared through a complex and long process and is given the right time to rest, ferment and become the delicious light product it is. We know firsthand that this is the result of long research hours and experiments done by Abdo himself for years. The quality of food is excellent yet at very fair prices. The ambiance is very homey and cozy. We recommend their Zaatar Baladi, simply delicious and light and really Baladi! We absolutely love their Sojok and Cheese and Beid bi Awerma, AMAZING! Halloum & Pesto Kaake is also recommended, the Kaake has a crunchy yet thin texture that you will love. Their Ayran with Thyme is really something, made of the simplest ingredients but worth every sip. This is a place developed with love, serving the best premium Lebanese products and we guarantee you will not regret your visit. Abdo, we are coming back soon! ;) || [4.0] [8 years ago on Zomato]: This was our first time at Abdo. I came here because I’ve heard so much about this place. After trying out the food thats ultimately amazing! The dough is great! I’ve asked the manager who’s very friendly how long had they been in the market and he told me 3 months in Hamra and 1 month in Gemmayze. They deserve a clap for bringing us great food and atmosphere in such a short time. I’m definitely coming back, the beid bi awarma was really good along with the pesto chicken. Thumbs up guys! || [5.0] [8 years ago on Zomato]: We were welcomed by Mr. Ghassan and M3alim Abdo to their Mar Mkhayel parlor! The place has 3 tables which seat 4 people each. The colors are soothing and simple; a lot of white, a bit of black and red! Mr. Abdo the honor is a vey humble man.  He was a history teacher who found his passion in making dough - in his free time; all kinds of dough. Let me assure you, he's an expert in dough! He uses no sugar, and lets the dough rest for 30 hrs making it lose 70% of the carbs. Lemonade: We had their fresh lemonade with ginger as we chatted. It was very refreshing and tasty no bitter or artificial taste whatsoever.  Then Mr. or M3alim Abdo suggested a few items to order. For starters we had: Zaatar Akhdar/Zoubaa'i  Freshly grinned thyme spread on a breathtaking dough with tomato and fresh mints; served with fresh veggies and labneh. Very enjoyable and light. Jebne and Sejok  Juicy sejok and melted cheese spread on that amazing dough served with tasty pickles. An orgasmic choice full of flavors. Next we ordered our mains: Pesto Chicken Wrap  Homemade pesto sauce so dear to my heart. The chicken was cooked to perfection and that dough was beyond amazing! A great combination! Burger Kaake  A kaake filled with a burger patty, sautéed fresh mushrooms and cheese! Very juicy, very tender and well balanced. It was amazing! The fries were perfectly fried and sprinkled with thyme. Club Kaake  Eggs, tomatoes, pickles, chicken and lettuce very fulfilling with a simple mustard sauce all stuffed into a small kaake. What's for dessert? Ka2ana Knefe  This is knefe goals! I'm in love! Stretchy cheese, crunchy osmalieh crust and just the right amount of qater and crushed pistachios! A must try !! Apple Crumble:  Served cold, this baby has apples seared, crumbles, pistachios and raisins. A more oriental version of the hot apple crumble served worldwide. M3alim Abdo you're a dough genius! We really enjoyed our dinner it was light yet fulfilling; filled with explosive flavors and a guilt free feel! We will be coming back soon; again and again! Highly recommended || [4.5] [8 years ago on Zomato]: Best thing about this place is that you can enjoy a dough-full breakfast without feeling "doughy" afterwards. Concept: Abdo is a former History teacher who, with time, discovered a passion for baking that resides deep inside of him. He then decided to pursue this passion by creating his very own “aged” dough, which is sugar-free, relying on the natural fermentation process of the yeast. Abdo’s dough needs around 24 hours to mature unlike the commercial dough which contains sugar to enhance the yeast’s feeding. Abdo stopped teaching history but history has never completely disappeared from his profession. Abdo processes a cozy venue with a simplicity that intrigues you first thing when you step into the restaurant. A chock board that lists the menu items and a counter behind which your food gets prepared on display, a couple of tables around which wooden chairs are placed, and the most basic and traditional tableware constituted of metallic dishes, glass jars for cups, metallic pans for plates, and guess what, their baskets are covered with papers that contain exerts from the history textbook. The whole thing tells a story on the behalf of M3allem Abdo which you’re lucky enough if you get to listen to its details from Abdo himself. Food: When science meets food, something like Abdo’s dough is created: a perfectly light bread that is easy to digest. This kind of bread is crafted with passion to meet high standards of production. I was surprised by the great taste of beef patty in a Kaake. That was something indescribable. There’s even a secret to the sauce which does not taste like your every day burger sauce but is still 100% Lebanese and homemade. The taste of the beef patty isn’t disguised in a sugar-loaded bun but rather in a light Kaake to make a magical burger! Thanks m3allem! I also tasted the Chicken Pesto wrap and this too was super special. It’s done with genuine Pesto sauce that is prepared in house. I couldn’t get enough of it! Also, don’t miss their sour Wild Zaatar saj and don’t miss the side Labne dip which is a mix of cow and goat Labne. Moving to desserts, Abdo’s creations are amazing! كأنها كنافة is going to become your next addiction. Even if you’re not a great knefe fan, you’re going to discover a new love: filled up with luscious cheese and covered with othmalieh. Ending up with it is a must! Don’t leave without trying it. Last but not least, if you’re craving a sweet indulgence, the Apple with Caramel is one great choice: a yummy mess of apples, caramel, cinnamon, and crumbs in a pot. Abdo restaurant is where you’d certainly feel at home; particularly in a house in the village where your grandparents reside. It’s so full of love and smiles. Recommended for breakfast, lunch, and a late night light dinner. I would like to thank the manager for his welcoming attitude, hospitality, good company, light conversation, and the smile that accompanied us from the moment we entered to the time we left. Thanks to M3allem Abdo for giving me the time for a small chat full of wisdom and genuineness. With love, Nour :) || [4.0] [8 years ago on Zomato]: The dough is reallyyyy good! High on that list for me, however, sits a Kaaket Knéféh Chmeliyyé with extra syrup: my most spiritually rewarding, overly delicious, 700-calorie temporary mistress my hands get to hold on a cold, rainy day at 8:00am in downtown Beirut. Chmeliyyé means northern, meaning the version done with ashta (clotted cream), as opposed to the Beirut-style, gooey cheese version. Enter Hallab in Hadat: think gloomy & sad without saying gloomy & sad. The last time I stepped into one, Fairuz was happy. A melange of fragrances, rose water, fresh coffee, pistachio paste and orange blossom once greeted your senses before the humans uttered anything. But today, the shrunken Borma portions and the absent Maamoul Madd trays spelled this branch's condolences to bucket lists like mine. No fault of the masters, folks. The country is in turmoil- and it is no time for bashing. But the utter, inexcusable, horrific injury to the once-upon-a-time soft, chewable semolina heritage: Microwaved Knefeh !! What ?! Foreign tourists: On behalf of the orthodox creameries and margarine-producing ancestors of this nation who are spinning in their graves, please accept our sincere condolences for a hallmark brand that just spelled its own demise. Please do not tell Rifaat. He competes, but he won't take this lightly, nor will Fouad El Jerr. Enough madness ! 1881 should be a UN resolution to rescind this branch's access to any form of sugar syrup. But wait, the kaaké survives. It's soft, the best at what it does yet lonely. Get your act together guys: don't mess with the knéféh. The microwave does not belong in shops like yours. || [4.0] [4 years ago on Google]: Very Tasty Sweets! And professional staff. name: Abo Harout
location: Byblos Roundabout, Byblos, Mount Lebanon Governorate, Lebanon
all_reviews: [4.0] [8 years ago on Zomato]: Been a customer of Abo harout for the past 8 years whenever I'm visiting Jbeil and I have the chance to pass by have a sandwich I do it, even if it is not eating time I pass by check on the owners who are a very welcoming friendly couple (husband,wife). I like their Sojok Omlet sandwich in Arabic bread I can barely finish my sandwich.. very tasty.. I've tried tawouk 3al fahem and Armenian kafta (with batenjen meshwe) also very tasty.. I love spicy food so tried also several times the special Armenian chili peppers paste added to my already spicy sandwich and it was pretty good...  Regular Sojok, Makanek sandwiches are also there..  food is tasty and "home made" considering being prepared by the owning couple... And extremely affordable sandwiches with big portions specially for the Arabic bread choice... on Sundays Abo Harout only sells whole sale raw products to homes and restaurants (example: 1kg raw Sojok.. etc..) he doesn't sell sandwiches on Sundays...  His raw products are from Mano (Burj hammoud)

Food: 5 Service: 5 Atmosphere: 4 || [5.0] [a year ago on Google]: From the outside it smells like a popular restaurant where the locals gather in a hidden corner. This is one of my theories about a good restaurant, before I try it I can smell something special and nice from the outside. In fact, the service is quite friendly and gentle, even to a stranger like me. One man explained the details of the dessert I had ordered and even prepared it in front of me before I started eating. A young lady kindly came over to ask me how I liked it. Some of the staff were busy on the floor but very attentive to each table. They seem to run a family business with a good team. The taste of the dishes is very simple but unique. As they focus on the special area like hummus where they are good at, their speciality is stronger and more specific than other restaurants. Also the special dessert, Debes bi Tahini, made of sesame and carob molasses which I had never tried before, but the taste was very thick and seemed healthy. The ambience is not very fancy, but cosy and homely. The bathroom, in particular, is very well maintained, including hand soap and paper towels. A lot of restaurants probably lack this level of hygiene, but this place has thought of every corner of the entire establishment. The prices aren't lower than in other ordinary restaurants, but given the level of quality, it's worth paying. # Beirut # Lebanon Service: Dine in Meal type: Lunch Food: 5 Service: 5 Atmosphere: 4 Vegetarian options: Hummus || [5.0] [a year ago on Google]: Lovely lovely place. name: Abou Mounir Fish Restaurant
location: VFXC+V63, Beirut, Beirut Governorate, Lebanon
all_reviews: [5.0] [5 months ago on Google]: When I was in Lebanon back in May, my brother-in-law insisted that I try Fish Market Abou Mounir because he knows how much I love seafood – and wow, wow, wow, what an experience! This place is truly a hidden gem and easily one of the best seafood spots I’ve ever been to. The location itself is perfect – it’s set right on the beach, so while you’re enjoying your meal, you also get to take in the beautiful sea view and fresh breeze. It creates the perfect Lebanese coastal atmosphere that immediately makes you feel relaxed and connected to the sea. The customer service was excellent from start to finish. The staff were warm, attentive, and very knowledgeable about their menu. They helped us choose from the wide selection of fresh fish displayed right in front of us. Everything is caught and prepared fresh, and you can tell the difference in every bite. Before diving into the seafood, we started with their Lebanese meza, and it was an absolute feast. We had hummus, tabbouli, fattoush, garlic potatoes, and a variety of small dishes that were bursting with flavor. Each dish was made with fresh ingredients and tasted exactly how traditional Lebanese meza should – light, vibrant, and full of flavor. Then came the seafood, and this is where Abou Mounir truly shines. We tried three or four different kinds of fresh fish, perfectly grilled and seasoned, along with calamari that was crispy, tender, and delicious. And the star of the table for me was the octopus – wow oh wow! It was grilled to perfection, tender yet smoky, and honestly one of the best I’ve ever had. What made the experience even more special was the way it ended. After the feast, they served us cookies along with Turkish delight – something we used to enjoy growing up in Lebanon. That small touch brought back so many wonderful memories and made the whole meal feel even more authentic and heartwarming. We basically tried a little of everything, and not a single dish disappointed. The portions were generous, the quality was top-notch, and the entire experience felt like a true Lebanese seafood celebration. If you’re in Lebanon and you love seafood, you cannot miss Fish Market Abou Mounir. Between the unbeatable beachfront location, the fresh catch of the day, the delicious Lebanese meza, and the warm hospitality, this place has it all. The pricing is reasonable considering the quality of food and service. It strikes a good balance, making it an accessible option for casual dining as well as special occasions. Another highlight is the dessert menu, which features traditional sweets like baklava and mouhalabieh that perfectly conclude your meal. Pairing them with a strong Lebanese coffee creates a wonderful finish to your dining experience. In summary, Abou Sami restaurant is a must-visit for anyone wanting to taste authentic Lebanese cuisine in a welcoming environment. Service: Delivery Meal type: Breakfast Food: 5 Service: 5 || [5.0] [2 years ago on Google]: Rajasthani cuisine is the cuisine of the Rajasthan region in North West India. It was influenced by various factors like the warlike lifestyles of its inhabitants, the availability of ingredients in an arid region and by Hindu temple traditions of sampradayas like Pushtimarg and Ramanandi.[1][2] Food that could last for several days and could be eaten without heating was preferred. Scarcity of water and fresh green vegetables have all had their effect on the cooking. Signature Rajasthani dishes include Dal Baati Churma, Panchratna Dal, Papad ro Saag, Ker Sangri,[3] Gatte ro Saag. It is also known for its snacks like Bikaneri bhujia, Mirchi bada and Kanda kachauri. Other famous dishes include Dal Baati, malaidar special lassi (lassi) and Lashun ki chutney (hot garlic paste), Mawa lassi from Jodhpur, Alwar ka mawa, Malpauas from Pushkar and rasgulla from Bikaner, "paniya"and "gheriya" from Mewar.[2] Originating for the Marwar region of the state is the concept Marwari Bhojnalaya, or vegetarian restaurants, today found in many parts of India, which offer vegetarian food of the Marwari people. The history also has its effect on the diet as the Rajputs preferred majorly a non-vegetarian diet while the Brahmin, Jains, and others preferred a vegetarian diet. So, the state has a myriad of both types of delicacies.[4] ￼Royal Rajasthani Thali served at Rambagh Palace, Jaipur According to a 2014 survey released by the registrar general of India, Rajasthan has 74.9% vegetarians, which makes it the most vegetarian state in India.[5] || [4.0] [3 years ago on Google]: Tried this place for takeaway . all_reviews: [4.0] [4 months ago on Google]: After several takeaways and dine-ins, I can honestly recommend this spot for a quick, tasty meal on the go. Located right in the heart of Dekweneh, it’s one of those local places that’s always buzzing — and for good reason. Their shawarma is the real deal. The chicken shawarma is tender, juicy, and has that perfect lemony-peppery balance that keeps you coming back. The beef shawarma is good too, though I personally lean toward the chicken — it’s just packed with flavor. The garlic paste hits a nice middle ground — smooth, light, and not overpowering. They also have their own special house sauce, which is interesting and worth trying, even if it’s not exactly my go-to flavor. If you’re into burgers, try their crispy chicken burger — it’s that Lebanese-style crispy burger we all love, golden-fried and crunchy with a hit of garlic. The broasted chicken platter is okay, but the breading reminds me a bit of Lebanese kaak, which isn’t quite my style for broasted — still, not bad overall. Service is quick, staff are friendly, and prices are very affordable. The place is usually lively, with outdoor seating that’s great for a casual bite with friends or a quick solo meal. Definitely one of those reliable Dekweneh spots to grab a shawarma fix or crispy bite anytime. Food: 4 Service: 5 Atmosphere: 4 || [1.0] [9 months ago on Google]: Worst shawerma ever. Whether you’re looking for a refined dining experience, a cozy garden retreat, or an unforgettable night of music, Agora in Ashrafiyeh delivers on all fronts. Sometimes the order arrives well-packed in a bag, other times there's no bag at all. The garlic sauce often leaks, which makes a mess. I hope they fix these issues, because the food can be really good when done right. The portions are big and then prices are so little according to the portions. The taste is always amazing. I recommend this place. || [3.5] [9 years ago on Zomato]: I've never sat and eaten at Al Walid, but I've ordered enough from them to know a thing or two about their fatteh bel laban and their hummus. First off, here's the thing about fatteh: there are so many variations of it. It's very uncommon to go to a place, try the fatteh, and find another place that has a fatteh that's similar to it. Some people do it with tahini, others with yogurt, others still with chicken, and some even do it with rice, and the ingredients and spices used are never quite the same. Some people even boil the yogurt. What the hell? So how would you describe fatteh to any person who's never had it? My answer would be: you show them the one served at Al Walid. Sure, there are better places to pick, but I can't tell you how many times I've ordered a fatteh at a big name restaurant and got served a bubbling abomination. People really seem to enjoy with this classic traditional dish for some reason. So back to Al Walid: their formula for the fatteh is tried and true. For one thing, you could clearly see the layers of fried bread, chick peas, yogurt, and fried cashews all neatly stacked on top of each other. For another, the amounts of each ingredient are just right; you'll never have to grab a fishing net to get the chick peas out of the bowl, and you'll never need to add extra yogurt to unbind the chick peas from the bread. And the garlic, a key ingredient that people usually get wrong, is ever so subtly present, with a shy yet inviting aroma. To top it all off, the portion's generous enough to satisfy anyone with less than four stomachs. It's not uncommon to see two persons splitting a bowl in half between themselves. I'll admit that I'm slightly less fond of their hummus, if only because there's nothing too special about it. Like the fatteh, it's more along the lines of tried and true, except here it's just one big bowl of plain hummus, so there aren't as many flavours involved. The upside is that you do get a 'service' with which you can liven up the taste of the hummus and make it an enjoyable meal on its own. On that note, I must say that it's a real shame that there are still restaurants out there that choose not to give service with something as simple as hummus! Nevertheless, Al Walid does have some problems. First, consistency: one look at the photos section and you'll know why. While the portion size is always fixed, on some days you might get less meat and cashews than normal, which is very annoying, considering that you're paying more money to have those in. Now, don't get me wrong, the prices are very palatable and actually rather cheap compared to other inferior joints, but when I ask for meat, I'd rather have its physical presence instead of only its smell, you know? And next week, everything's fine again... but maybe not the week after! The second problem Al Walid has is with the service. The tomato slices have never looked appetizing to me, but the mint can be especially troublesome. I like to chew some mint after meals like this, but sometimes I get a tuft of dusty, dirty mint leaves that crack and grind against my teeth and it just leaves me upset. Would I recommend the place? Sure, go ahead and try the fatteh, but don't forget to be specific with your order to ensure that you don't get some unpleasant surprises. Also, a tip: ask them to pack the fried bread separately. || [4.0] [9 years ago on Zomato]: This is a great place to order a Lebanese breakfast from. We have Foul Fridays at work and we always order from Al Walid. The fatteh, eggs, hummus, msabaha, and foul are all very good! They deliver quickly and it's so cheap! The portions are veryy generous. We always always have a lot left order no matter how hard we try. When a ordering a tip is to order only one or two of your items with bread and veggies and the rest without because you will end up with too much veggies left over it's really a waste. Service: Delivery Food: 5 Service: 5 Atmosphere: 5 || [5.0] [7 months ago on Google]: Food: 5 Service: 5 Atmosphere: 5 || [5.0] [7 months ago on Google]: While I was in Beirut doing some shopping for the family, I started to feel hungry and decided to try a place I hadn’t been to before—Arouset Labneh. I’m so glad I did because it turned out to be an excellent choice. It was me, my brother, and two of our friends. I had my eye on the beef shawarma, while my brother went for the chicken shawarma. Our friends decided to skip the platters and try the sandwiches instead. From the moment we walked in, the service stood out. Our server—whose name I sadly forgot—was friendly, attentive, and made us feel welcome from the start. One thing I really appreciated was the presentation. The shawarma platters were served on wooden trays, giving the meal a rustic, stylish touch. Everything was neatly arranged, and you could tell they put effort into making the food look as good as it tasted. The beef shawarma was flavorful, tender, and perfectly seasoned, with just the right amount of spices to give it that authentic Lebanese taste. The chicken shawarma was equally delicious—juicy, well-marinated, and paired perfectly with the sauces and sides. But it’s not just shawarma that Arouset Labneh does well—their kabobs are outstanding. Both the beef and lamb kabobs were juicy, tender, and packed with flavor. The fact that everything here is made to order makes a huge difference in taste and freshness. You can really tell the meat is grilled fresh for each customer, giving it that perfect smoky aroma and tenderness. The atmosphere of the restaurant added to the experience—it had a clean, modern feel but still carried a warm and inviting vibe. Combined with the great service, it made for a relaxing and enjoyable meal. Overall, Arouset Labneh was a fantastic find. Between the delicious shawarma, mouthwatering kabobs, beautiful presentation, and excellent service, it’s the kind of place I’d happily return to the next time I’m in Beirut. Whether you’re in the mood for a full platter or just a quick sandwich, you’re going to leave satisfied. Rating: – Great taste, great service, and everything made fresh to order. all_reviews: [3.5] [9 years ago on Zomato]: I love the concept. Attila is the only Mongolian cuisine and it's a pity that you only find it at a food court. You choose your raw ingredients then take them to the chef who cooks them on a huge grill and you get served. Cool idea! Not the fanciest of plate presentations. The quality was good it could be better if the portion of noodles wasn't too big in relation to the meat and fish! The last time though it was a disappointment because I tried the vermicelli noodles and turned out to have a bad taste. I hope they would open their own restaurant outside the mall for a better experience. || [3.0] [9 years ago on Zomato]: A very nice concept that got ruined in a food court and made it cheaper. The way you pick up the ingredients and make your own dish of seafood, meat, chicken or a mix of any is one of a kind here. You pick up your food when it's done and for a weird reason the food has no taste. Maybe by putting many things altogether, the flavors get messed up. I don't think I would try them out again unless they open their own restaurant and introduce some real Mongolian food. || [3.5] [9 years ago on Zomato]: So first of all here are my 5 criteria chart for a food court: - Food Quality - Price - Staff and service - design and originality - Menu options 1) Food Quality: We have ordered an "Attila Normal size meal" and a " Chicken Normal size meal"; the Attila meal was delightful and full of fresh vegetables such as broccoli, mushrooms, green pepper, baby corn, onion both green leaves and the actual onion. The attila meal contains shrimps, chicken and meat. YES ALL THREE. And since i personally love trying such an awkward mix, i went directly towards it. The meal is prepared on a large mongolian plate grill which directly attracts the eyesight, and i'll tell you how it's prepared; they first heat the veggies aforementioned using a splash of olive oil, then they add the choice of meat/chicken/shrimp, and a foreign chef will start chopping them. He finally adds Noodles and your choice of sauce ( i'll be talking about that later ). When it's all done, the chef puts the whole stuff in a plastic bowl and adds rice on the side. This is pretty much it. Nothing too fancy. The two meals were very fine and a normal size would satisfy a normal person's hunger. The chicken meal runs through the same process and honestly, they both taste pretty much the same. I'll mark 0.7/1 here. 2) Price: The price of the chicken meal was 11.5$ while the Attila mix costs 13.3$. And i would resay this, this was a normal size meal, and would fill the hunger of any normal person. To feel full for 13.3$ and get to eat a healthy meal, this is something worth stating clearly. This place offers in my opinion value for money. 0.9/1 here. 3) Staff and service: As any typical food court, you don't get much in contact with any of the workers, waiters or pretty much any person besides the cashier person; however, a welcoming lady was standing infront of the place greeting everyone trying to catch new customers. She was the sweetest person, throwing jokes and saying good stuff, promising us she'll take special care of our order and says she'll bring the food to us once it's done. She helped us pick up from around 15 sauce bowls since we did not understand what they were and she was more knowledgeable. I'll mark a 0.9/1 here also 4)Design and originality: This place is the most attractive one between all the food court of Beirut City Center. It's located directly on the right and with the big round mongolian grill and the huge plate which makes a sound when an order has done. Huge attraction. 1/1 5)Menu Options: Here is what dissapointed me the most. Menu options. The main dishes are only the chicken , meat, seafood and Attila mix while the rest of the menu is just appetizers. This doesn't leave much of a choice for a customer. 0.2/1 here. Reminder: This rating is only for the place being in a food court, and i would judge the restaurant based on many other criteria if it was not. Conclusion: This place is a healthy meal's place, a place to eat and feel good of urself, and actually not pay alot. Total points: 3.7/5 --> 3.5/5 Averagely good. Thank you Attila, Thank you All. || [5.0] [9 years ago on Facebook]: Amazing place to visit and enjoy food  Good job thx || [5.0] [9 years ago on Facebook]: Best food Iv tasted now I order everytime I visit city center with family and friends || [1.5] [9 years ago on Zomato]: It was my first visit there. the staff was welcoming and sweet but she didn't provide us any details about the food and sauces. The chicken is incredibly fresh, cooked to perfection whether it’s grilled over charcoal or served crispy. The charcoal chicken has that authentic smoky flavor that’s hard to beat, while the crispy version is golden, crunchy on the outside, and juicy on the inside. Their shawarma — both chicken and beef — is absolutely delicious, packed with rich spices and balanced textures. Every bite reflects quality ingredients and skillful preparation. Presentation is always on point, and the flavors are bold, clean, and deeply satisfying. The variety of cold mezzeh — from hummus to mutabbal — is super fresh and full of taste, making the perfect addition to any meal. The service is fast and friendly, and the menu offers great variety without compromising quality. It’s rare to find a place that serves fast food with this much heart and attention to detail. Highly recommended if you’re looking for authentic Lebanese flavors with a modern, high-quality twist. Truly one of the best fast food experiences in Lebanon! The combination of the excessively loud music and the staff's apparent amusement at our expense made the atmosphere feel more like a social gathering rather than a professional dining establishment. To make matters worse, when we received the bill, it felt inflated, further diminishing our overall experience. We have been patrons of this establishment numerous times before, but after two consecutive disappointing visits, we have decided not to return. While the prices were already on the higher side, the unique experiences we used to enjoy made it worth it. Unfortunately, recent occurrences have led us to conclude that this is no longer the case. I love it when comments are taken into consideration and feedback are important to the restaurant. They called us back and when we told them our feedback, they sent us the same burgers that wee ordered and a goat cheese salad even though they didnt have to. The staff on the phone is extremely nice and charming. They just called back to get our feedback from this time and the owner told us how important reviews are to him and how they took every comment into consideration. The 4.5 is especially for that amazing customer care. ● Order: Southern burger, Bbq burger and goat cheese salad. The burgers were as good to our eyes than to our taste buds. Tons of melted cheese were all over the burgers and a generous amount of sauce too. My favorite one was the Bbq burger, even though the meat is not special there, the overall bite is good. The meat and chicken were cooked to perfection this time. And now, for the goat cheese salad: Thumbs up!! A very original and fresh salad that is topped with a unique sauce created by the owner of the place. Some of the goat cheese balls were mixed with semson and other with pistachio! Loved it. Prices are very affordable and the portions are fulfilling! OLD REVIEW: ---------------- ●Delivery experience: The staff was friendly on the phone and was very professional and good to us when they realized they forgot to send us an extra bread with the mix platter. They called back and looked for solutions then when we told them that we werent going to eat it so its okay they said they were going to call later to get a feedback. The delivery time was okay, not fast but not slow. Their packagings are great and the menu is well designed. The order was accurate but the burgers were inverted even though they wrote the names on the package! Prices are very affordable (bill of 45LL for 3 people) ● Order: mixed grilled platter, Southern burger, Bbq burger, Veggie guaccamole sandwich. I must say that I expected a bit more of the taste when I saw the pictures, the great food presentation, the professionalism on the phone and the good packaging. The chicken and meat were undercooked in the burgers when they were supposed to be "grilled" and the taouk was too hard to chew and did not taste like one. Upon arrival today, we were told by the front desk lady that the poolside was “fully booked.” Out of trust, we accepted an alternative table by the beach. However, while heading to the WC and passing by the pool, I noticed that over 20 poolside seats were mostly empty with only 4 guests seated. I immediately asked to speak with the person in charge. The first explanation given was: “We cannot seat groups of 8 at the pool.” I offered a simple solution: we can be split as 2 groups or 4 couples—it’s still the same total of 8 seats and we’re fine with separate or single billing. Still, they declined. Then, a second excuse was presented: “There is a minimum charge at the pool.” This was never communicated to us at the time of booking. I made it clear that if such a policy existed, we should have been informed in advance so we could make a proper decision. No one mentioned this when we called to reserve. When we returned to our beachside seating, another staff member approached us with the same inconsistent reasoning, then added something even more surprising: “The minimum charge is up to me, and I can apply it at any time before, during, or after service.” Frankly, this was unprofessional, unclear, and completely arbitrary. We’ve visited BDP many times before and always made it a point to arrive early to choose good seating before the place gets busy. This time, even with plenty of empty poolside seating and a confirmed request, we were refused without any valid justification. No solution was provided. Just excuse after excuse. We were left very disappointed with how poorly this was handled. We hope the management takes this feedback seriously. Recommend to be tried. || [4.5] [9 years ago on Zomato]: Can one be addicted to Hot Dogs and Nachos?? We would like to thank BRGR.CO management for receiving us along with a group of sweet foodies for a tasting session of some additions made on their new menu. It wasn’t our first time at BRGR.CO actually, the place is still gorgeous and very pleasantly decorated and their service remains unchanged: Superior is the best word to describe servicing in this “casual” American fast food We were served: • New York style salad: a “passe-par-tout” salad that should play to all tastes, • Golden chicken filets: Unlike most chicken strips, chicken tenders available over the Lebanese market, these chicken filet starters are very similar to escalope and are super tasty. They take very little frying oil and remain crispy. • Nachos: Among the BEST! Topped with double layers of Swiss and cheddar chesses and served with jalapeno, a succulent salsa, a very nice guacamole, and an outstanding sour cream. This nacho starter is really to DIE FOR. • Coney Island Dog: NEVER MISS THIS ONE. Rarely found over the Lebanese market, this pure American style hot dog is made of sausage topped with corn carne like ground meat sauce, yellow mustard and raw white onion cubes... add twenty times the word “very” followed by the word “delicious”. • Fish and chips: a good coating and frying for this sea bass filet.. The side fries were super tasty as well. • Fries and Cheese: all two thumbs up for this plate, one of our all-time favorite at BRGR.CO Now the burgers: • A Mushroom Swiss burger and a Portobello and Smokey Burger: Both delicious but leave it them to the end of your meal.  Portobello is a very light mushroom in terms of calories however very peculiar in terms of cooking and taste. One hint to be given here is NEVER to smell the mushroom or it will take a while before being able to taste anything else. We did it ourselves and we kept eating and all food ended up tasting and feeling the mushroom taste. • Truffled Provolone burger: The bestie in terms of acceptance by foodies and clients, an easy going one loaded with flavors, specifically provolone cheese. • A veggie Portobello mushroom burger: in one word; not for us. Portobello is very delicate when it comes to cooking and has loads of taste in every bite. The Portobello veggie burger proposed by BRGR.CO comprises of a full Portobello, like the whole mushroom! However, a smoother recipe might do the job like a combination of chopped Portobello, black beans, assorted herbs, very little garlic, would also deliver a hearty tasty goodness for veggie burgers lovers while reducing / hindering the strong taste of Portobello. Now the deserts: • Minnesotta Fudge: Mouthwatering from A to Z... A straight Recommendation for us • Caramel and date Pudding: Amazingly prepared with heavenly tastes if one is used to pudding! • White chocolate cheesecake: the least tasty among all three deserts but does the job if tasted alone or before the fudge and the pudding In sum, BRGR.CO ABC Ashrafieh, is a very plausible place for a causal High end American Fast food experience where one would enjoy tasty / addictive meals in a relaxed setting. || [4.5] [9 years ago on Zomato]: Location 9/10 in Level 3 in ABC Store ! all_reviews: [5.0] [6 months ago on Google]: @burgerlayover Buco open its doors at the end of 2023 by chef Tarek who is ex-sous chef at Noma in Denmark. Their main courses are mostly burgers and they do have amazing starters for those who don’t feel like having burgers. We ordered the naked wagyu with hakkaido milk bun, 200gr wagyu meat and provolone cheese. The miso bbq with 200gr Australian grain-fed, mature cheddar cheese, jalapeños, pickled red cabbage & homemade miso BBQ sauce, Hokkaido milk bun. And Mulberry bacon with 200gr Australian grain-fed, pork bacon, cheddar cheese, crispy onion, mulberry jam, Dijon mustard, potato bun. They all served with very well fried french fries. Both the wagyu and the australian grain-fed meats were super fresh and juicy. My fav was the mulberry burger comes on a soft, shiny potato bun that gives a nice pillowy bite without overshadowing the fillings. The Australian grain-fed beef delivers a rich, meaty flavor, juicy and tender, which pairs well with the sharp cheddar melting into it. The crispy onion adds a satisfying crunch to break through the softness, while the pork bacon layers in a smoky, salty punch. What really makes this burger stand out, though, is the mulberry jam—sweet and slightly tart, it balances the richness of the meat and cheese beautifully, creating that sweet-salty contrast that keeps you going back for another bite. The Dijon mustard sneaks in just enough sharpness to cut through the sweetness and fat, tying everything together. Overall: this burger is indulgent yet balanced, with a clever twist from the jam. The bun holds well, the textures play nicely, and every bite hits salty, sweet, tangy, and crunchy notes. The fries on the side look golden and crisp, served stylishly in a martini glass—definitely adds to the presentation. Staff are great and loved the atmosphere. The restaurant excels in every aspect, offering a unique dining experience that sets it apart from typical seafood establishments. One of the standout features of Babel Bahr is their authentic charcoal-grilled fish, a method that truly caters to the discerning palate of gourmet enthusiasts. Unlike the common gas or electric grilling methods, the use of real charcoal imparts a distinctive flavor and aroma to the fish that elevates the dining experience. For an authentic taste of the region, I highly recommend trying the local fish varieties such as Ajaj, Braq, or Gazaleh, which are seasonal delights typically available until late summer. These fresh catches truly showcase the rich flavors of Lebanese seafood. To complement your grilled fish, be sure to pair it with a glass of their excellent local Arak. The combination of grilled fish and Arak creates a harmonious blend of flavors that is sure to delight your taste buds. Overall, Babel Bahr offers a culinary experience that is not to be missed for seafood aficionados looking to savor the best of Lebanese fish cuisine. From the charcoal-grilled delicacies to the superb local drinks, a visit to Babel Bahr promises a memorable dining experience. If you're indecisive of your choice of dish, ask for the menu of Prune & Acoté. P.S: don't forget the pain retrouvé of Prune for dessert. || [3.0] [10 years ago on Zomato]: Newest addition to the duo Acoté & Prune, Bar Jamón, as the name indicates, specializes in Spanish ham. Cozy decoration essentially made of wood. With pieces of deli suspended in the air or put on a stand, as every place of the type should do. I went there for a drink, and to try the ham of course. After taking a look at the menu, I had to notice that for a place with Jamón in its name, only having five types of deli, three of cheese and four of wine is certainly not enough, when it is know that every region in Spain specializes in its own varieties and grades of these items. We tried the tabla mixa plate ( 2 cheese, 2 jamon), which tasted good and fresh. However, as the prices may seem fair on the menu, the 'minúsculo' portions we received clearly indicate otherwise. I can roughly say that for 36 000LL, we received around 60g of cheese and 60g of deli. I know that these restaurants have to cope with Mar Mkhael's extravagant rents, but this is clearly too much. The wine tasted good, and the waiters proposal of ordering from either Acoté or Prune as well is a nice option. All in all, Bar Jamón is a pretty good place for people looking to enjoy a  quiet sitting around drinks and snacks, but not the one for ham lovers looking to satisfy their appetite. || [5.0] [10 years ago on Zomato]: Located in Mar Mkhael, Bar Jamon is a Spanish restaurant offering the best Spanish cuisine. The quality of the food along with the whole experience were excellent. I would definitely go again and recommend it to all my friends and family. || [N/A] [10 years ago on Foursquare]: Grat food , great mood , nice wine || [4.0] [10 years ago on Zomato]: Bar Jamon is a newly opened place in mar mkhael where you can chill and enjoy some cold cuts and cheeses! I recommend trying the awesom sangrias, one of the best in beirut! || [4.5] [10 years ago on Zomato]: From the makers of French bistrot Prune and gourmet sandwicherie Acoté comes Spanish tapas spot Bar Jamon nestled right next door to its predecessors. The food is delicious and a typical scene right out of Spain, complete with pata negra, sheep's milk Manchego cheese, sobrassada on wholewheat bread, and patatas bravas. The red wine sangria will help you wash it all down, so be sure to order a pitcher. Hopefully Bar Jamon will soon feature authentic Spanish desserts, but in the meantime, the pain retrouvé from Prune fills in marvelously. Note that prices don't really fit the bar backdrop (expect around $100 per couple with drinks). But trust me, it'll be a satisfying meal well worth your Benjamin. || [4.5] [10 years ago on Zomato]: Love the concept of wine and tapas.. From the welcome by the more tha friendly staff to the presentation of the plates to the portions/prices this place sets a new standard for surrounding resto-pubs || [3.5] [10 years ago on Zomato]: We were invited to Bar Jamon for a Zomato Foodie MeetUp! The place is located in Mar Mikhael on the same strip as Acoté and Prune! Sipping on a glass of white wine our evening with our fellow foodies began. We started with the Tabla de Quesos a plate of cheese with slices of fresh baguette and the Tabla de Jamon (Chorizo, Salchichon Iberico and Jamon Serrano). We loved the cheese plate and the baguette was warm and soft. We loved the presentation of The Grilled Asparagus & Serrano but found it to be too salty! Our favorite was the Salpicon de pulpo which is marinated octopus, the flavor and taste were really good. The Gambas Al Ajillo were a bit too oily but tasted good with the chili and garlic marination. The patatas bravas were good but the aioli sauce tasted more like sweet mayonnaise which ruined the taste of the potatoes. The Manchego, ruccola & truffle oil was really good the ruccola & truffle oil compliment the manchego cheese very well. We loved the Sobrassada (pork sausage), goat cheese and honey but the bread was too hard to cut or bite into it would have been better in a much softer bread. The tostada Serrano & Manchego was also delicious. For Blue Cheese Lovers The Beetroot & Blue Cheese Salad is a must! The Artichokes and Manchego Cheese Salad was really good. Overall the place is very cozy with friendly service and good vibes. || [4.5] [10 years ago on Zomato]: The closest to Spanish cuisine i had in Lebanon. Good sangria, patatas bravas, jamón and cheese || [3.0] [10 years ago on Zomato]: A new Spanish restaurant “Bar Jamón” has popped up in the Mar Mikhael sector and we had the chance to try it firsthand thanks to Zomato. Bar Jamon’s concept differs from its neighboring sisters À Côté and Prune and is rather new to Beirut as well. The bar specializes in Spanish cheese, jamon (ham) and traditional Spanish tapas. The Tabla de Quesos presented Manchego cheese (sheep) and Mahon cheese (cow) served with sweet tomato jam and warm slices of soft white baguette. The Tabla de Jamon presented Jamon Serrano, spicy Chorizo and Salchichon Iberico served with tomato sauce, cornichons (baby pickles / gherkins) and warm white baguette slices. The chorizo and salchichon were too tough to eat. The Tapas: 	Grilled Asparagus & Serrano: These were very tasty but I found them very salty. 	Salpicon de Pulpo (Octopus Salad): This was my favourite of all the offered tapas! 	Gambas al Ajillo (Garlic Prawns): The shrimps were not plump and juicy, but the sauce, however oily, was tasty (“kezebra w toum” style). It would have been great to have warm bread served with this dish so we can dip it in the sauce. When the shrimps were finished, the plate was taken away before we enjoyed using the sauce the traditional way! 	Patatas Bravas: Fried potato cubes drizzled with a sweet mayonnaise dressing. The smaller potato cubes were too crunchy as opposed to being crispy with a fluffy interior due to the excess oil in the frying process. In Spain, this tapa is known to be one of the spiciest dishes. In my opinion, it could be much better. Maybe have them served with a spicy tomato sauce as another option. 	Manchego, Ruccola & Truffle Oil: A slate with a large slice of Manchego cheese with a drizzle of truffle oil and some ruccola garnish. It is a good simple dish, but again it needed to be served with some bread! 	Sobrassada, Goat Cheese & Honey: Toasted slices of baguette spread with spiced sobrasada (ground pork sausage) and topped with goat cheese and honey and a rather messy ruccola leaves garnish. I really liked the flavour combination; the honey complemented the goat cheese perfectly. However, my dinner companions and I found the bread very chewy. 	Tostada Serrano & Manchego: These “bruschettas” of serrano ham and manchego cheese were average; I would have added some Dijon mustard for more flavour and a spicy kick. 	Beetroot & Blue Cheese Salad: If you are a blue cheese lover, then this salad is for you; I am not a fan so I steered clear. 	Artichokes & Manchego Cheese Salad: I liked the combination of the grilled artichoke hearts and the manchego cheese, but I found that the crispy fried parsley (?) sprinkled on top was flavourless and had an unappetizing texture. Bar Jamon’s menu consists of small plates (tapas) for roughly 2 persons to share; maximum 3 if you are ordering many dishes. Dessert should be added to the menu or at least mentioned on a blackboard. The décor is warm and cozy with great friendly service. If you are looking for something outstanding and tasty (and not Spanish) in Mar Mikhael, I would really encourage you to try À Côté (Bar Jamon’s sister). It is one of my favourite venues in the area; I strongly recommend the goat cheese salad and their “Pain Retrouvé”. || [4.0] [10 years ago on Zomato]: Sometimes we get bored from ordinary food and we search for something new and special. Neither burgers nor pizzas nor sushi but something simple and won't make you feel guilty yet delicious. Our coup de coeur was Bar Jamón in Mar Mikhael next to Acoté in Mar Mkhael. It is specialised in cheese, charcuterie, tapas and of course jamon. The place is very relaxing and positive where you can enjoy drinks with yummy bites. The menu offers Spanish items focusing on some delicious and unique cold cuts that you won't find anywhere else. • We started our dinner with Sobrassada, Goat Cheese and Honey. It consisted of pork sausage with creamy goat cheese, honey and rocket leaves. It was the best and yummiest thing we have tried since some time. No words can describe how perfect it was. You must try it and discover by yourselves. • The Patatas Bravas were addictive. It's made of crispy cubed potatoes with garlic allioli sauce. We finished them in a minute. • The Avocado, Smoked Salmon and Ikura is the healthy dish by excellence. It contained some premium quality smoked salmon slices with half an avocado filled with fish eggs (Ikura). We loved how fresh it was and full of vitamins! • The Manchego, Ruccola and truffle oil contained our favorite ingredients. It was very simple yet excellent. It was the first time we tried this cheese and we loved it. Don't forget to ask for bread for full indulgence. • The succulent Beetroot and Blue Cheese salad consisted of the green leaves were extremely fresh and the sauce was perfect! Blue cheese lovers this is a plate not to be missed. The octopus dish was a winner, we loved how fresh it was and the it was well marinated. We never finish a food experience without desserts. We ordered a Chocolat Mou and a Double Chocolate Dose cake. • The Chocolat Mou was amazing. We all know what it contains but the quality of the whipped cream and the ice cream used made it divine. • The Double Chocolate Dose cake was orgasmic. The cake was moist and kind of creamy as if it was not fully baked, with a chocolate ice cream on top that made it even more moist. Like you see, the platters we talked about had non ordinary and few ingredients yet the taste was succulent! Bar Jamón should be on your restaurant list because if you don't pay them a visit you will be really missing out. || [4.5] [10 years ago on Zomato]: Had a wonderful night at Bar Jamon today! Started off our dinner with a glass of Sangria and a Tabla de Quesos (cheese platter) and a Tabla de Jamon (jamon platter of chorizo, salchichon and lomo Iberico) with slices of bread. Great starters! We then shared: - Manchego, ruccola and truffle oil - Tostada serrano & manchego - Beetroot & blue cheese salad - Gambas Al Ajillo And my top 5 were: - Grilled Asparagus & Serrano which were a bit too salty but still real delicious - Salpicon de pulpo - Sobrassada, goat cheese & honey - Patatas bravas - Artichokes and manchego cheese To top it all we even got the chance to cut out the Jamon at a live station Amazing Spanish experience !! Much recommended! || [4.0] [10 years ago on Zomato]: Thanks to zomato for the invitation tonight to this new place place in the middle of mar mkhayel.. As for the food, it’s delicious the daily dishes and the Lebanese salads were perfect. It’s decent and the portions are fair, but overall not outstanding. We had a variety of plates: -Cellar’s Melted Cheese Nachos: Very basic and they came alone without any dips, we thought it is how they serve them. When we were halfway into them, they got the dips. -Cellar’s Sweet Chilli Calamari: These were a light and good menu, nothing special though. -Eggplant Parmiggiano Melanzane: Not a big fan of eggplant, so I don’t usually enjoy this plate. The people who like it said it was fine. -Crab Salad: It was fine as well, the quality of the crab was good. -Spaghetti Carbonara: This was my favorite item. It was super delicious, and if I were to go there again, I would definitely have it. -Penne Arrabiata: This literally had no taste. -Lemon Veal Scalopine: This was very basic. I had to mix it with the pasta to enjoy it a bit. -Pain Perdu: My favorite dessert. It was really good, but what made us laugh was that we got served half a Pain Perdu that is usually served in other places (check the picture to see what I mean). -Chocolate Fondant: This was also good, but super tiny. -Profiteroles: These were also a good option. Meal type: Dinner Food: 5 Service: 5 Atmosphere: 5 || [5.0] [10 months ago on Google]: Beit Kanz is one of those rare places I find myself returning to over and over again—not just for the food, but for the feeling it evokes every time I walk through its doors. It’s more than a restaurant; it’s a home. A sanctuary that captures the essence of Beirut in its most beautiful, soulful form. Nestled in the iconic Tabbal building in Ashrafieh, Beit Kanz is a story of resilience and beauty. After the devastating port blast in 2020, the building was restored with such care and love that it now stands as a testament to Beirut’s ability to heal and shine again. The renovation preserved its architectural soul, and stepping inside feels like being embraced by Beirut’s rich heritage. Every visit is a journey. From the warm service to the comforting aromas of authentic Lebanese dishes, everything is crafted with intention and heart. The food is simply exceptional—flavors that remind you of home, prepared with generosity and served with genuine care. And what makes the experience even more meaningful is the spirit behind it: Beit Kanz is powered by Beit El Baraka, a foundation dedicated to supporting the community, which gives your meal a deeper sense of purpose. The staff are not only professional but deeply kind and attentive, making every guest feel like a long-lost friend. Whether you’re seated indoors, surrounded by vintage Beiruti charm, or enjoying the fresh air in the garden, you’re transported to a Beirut that feels timeless. Beit Kanz is more than a dining spot. It’s a reminder of who we are, where we come from, and how even in difficult times, beauty, tradition, and kindness continue to thrive. The bread was served with an odd yogurt dip. The food slowly started arriving. The appetizer was the tricolor cake, grilled eggplant, halloumi cheese, sundried tomatoes, and pesto sauce. The *burger* stood out—it was *tasty, with juicy and flavorful meat*. However, the *coleslaw on the side was not fresh*, which was disappointing . The *Philadelphia steak* was another highlight, well-balanced with its sauce and meat. The *chicken truffle sandwich*, served on a *full grain sub*, was unfortunately a letdown. The *bread itself made the chicken feel even drier*, and the truffle flavor wasn’t prominent enough to elevate the dish. The dense, hearty sub overpowered the delicate elements, making the overall experience underwhelming. A change in bread choice and a juicier preparation could really improve this item. One of the *most frustrating aspects* was the *playground policy*. The entry fee is $5, whether indoors or outdoors, which is understandable for managing the space. However, during the indoor experience, there was *no supervision for 30 minutes*, which was concerning. If guests are paying for the playground service, there should be *better oversight* to ensure safety. A more effective strategy could be offering *free access* to the playground for guests who spend above a certain amount on food and drinks. This would encourage families to enjoy their time without feeling burdened by an extra charge. Overall, Bugs Cafe has potential, but *improvements in food selection and service strategy* could make the experience much better.
